100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On March 14, 1926, the British Parliament faced mounting pressure as the coal miners' strike continued, threatening to disrupt the nation's energy supply. In the United States, the Senate debated a bill aimed at regulating the burgeoning radio industry, signaling a growing recognition of the medium's impact on society. Meanwhile, in Germany, political tensions escalated as the government struggled to address economic instability following the post-war reparations.
Updated: Saturday, March 14, 2026 at 07:20:26 AM CDT
